extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro won uphill sprint finish at La Molina after THOMAS Geraint's acceleration with 2km remaining failed to sustain a gap and reset the group.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint attacked with 2km remaining but immediately soft-pedaled, revealing Sky's strategy to fragment rhythm rather than commit sustained efforts early.
VALVERDE Alejandro positioned behind teammate on final approach, using Movistar leadout to mark MARTIN Dan's early sprint before unleashing superior uphill power.
CONTADOR Alberto used PANTANO Jarlinson pacemaking on earlier climb sections, revealing Trek's willingness to commit domestiques before final kilometer.
FROOME Chris unable to respond to THOMAS and KENNAUGH accelerations, signaling below-peak form in first stage race since previous year.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: demonstrated elite uphill sprint power after 5hr stage, marked MARTIN Dan perfectly and kicked decisively in final 200m
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: strong enough to attack with 2km out but tactically chose fragmentation over sustained effort, showing controlled aggression
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: initiated uphill sprint too early (several hundred meters out), lacked explosive power to hold off VALVERDE in final kick
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: dropped by THOMAS and KENNAUGH accelerations, confirmed deliberately under-peak form in season buildup phase
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: neutral: positioned well with PANTANO support on earlier climbs but no decisive move attempted on final ascent
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: lost 22 seconds on stage finish, unable to stay with select group over final climb
